name: Ragout De Pattes Et De Boulettes- Petit Pouc
recipeInstructions:
How to make Brown Flour:
Spread all-purpose flour in a large, heavy frying pan and heat over medium heat, stirring, until flour turns a medium brown. May be refrigerated for several months.
How to make Oatmeal Flour:
Oatmeal flour may be made by pulverizing rolled oats in a food processor. All-purpose flour may be substituted.
Ragout de Pattes:
1. Place pork hocks in a large heavy pot with onion, bay leaf, savory, thyme, and cloves. Cover with cold water, bring to a boil, skim off any scum from the surface, then reduce heat and simmer very slowly for 2 - 2.5 hours or until pork hocks are tender. Remove meat from bouillon and set aside. Strain bouillon and skim off fat.
2. Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add browned flour and cook, stirring, until the mixture bubbles all over.
3. Gradually add strained bouillon while continuously stirring. Cook until the mixture thickens. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
4. Return meat hocks to the saucepan. Add cooked meatballs and simmer gently for about 30 minutes. Season again with salt and pepper.
Boulettes:
1. Combine ground pork, onion, garlic, cinnamon, cloves, salt, pepper, crumbled bread, and beaten egg in a bowl. Mix well and add just enough flour to make the mixture firm.
2. Shape into meatballs, about 1 - 1.25 inches in diameter.
3. Grease a large heavy frying pan and brown the meatballs on all sides over medium heat. Continue cooking on low heat for about 15 minutes. Makes 12 - 15 meatballs.
From the Petit Poucet Restaurant.
